**Borderless Radiologist: Incubation Workflow**

*Platform integration, n8n pipeline, grading, and the Borderless decision*

# **1\. Why this workflow exists**

We are launching a new tier, Borderless Radiologist, for high-trust, high-commitment radiologists. Before a candidate enters the tier and starts reading real patient cases under the Borderless brand, we need to verify two things. Their reporting quality against a known reference. And their reliability against the slot commitments the tier requires.

The 7-day incubation does that verification through a fixed pool of 80 historical cases we already hold ground-truth reports for. At the end, one of four outcomes: eligible for Borderless perks, or not, for a specific reason.

This document describes what you are building, why each piece exists, and what the radiologist sees on the other end of it. If you finish reading this and you understand the radiologist's experience as well as the code paths, the doc has done its job.

# **2\. What already exists vs. what you are building**

Two engineering surfaces are in scope: the reporting platform and n8n. Confirm the left column is in place before starting.

| Already exists (use as-is) | You are building |
| :---- | :---- |
| The 80-case pool: study\_iuids, complex / non-complex tags, case metadata, and ground-truth report references | On reporting platform: webhook integration that fires to n8n only when role \= borderless\_rad AND incubation \= true |
| Production DB containing submitted reports | On reporting platform: the decision API that n8n calls when incubation concludes (§8.3) |
| DICOM anonymization script (callable from the pipeline) | On reporting platform: notification UI for suspension (§7.4) and for each of the four decision outcomes (§8.5) |
| LLM grading prompt (a minor tweak needed, see §6) | n8n: one-time pool setup. Download DICOMs, anonymize, upload |
| Punctuality / Regularity / Consistency tracking (slot-commitment data) | n8n: all per-rad state. Case count, uniqueness (no case assigned twice), pool pointer |
| Radiologist reporting platform with Start Reporting / Submit-and-Next actions | n8n: case-push state machine, async grading, 20-case gate, terminal decision |
| Ops Slack workspace | n8n: milestone alerts to ops Slack at the 20-case gate and at terminal |
|  | Shared: the audit table storing per-case grading results (§10) |

The key split. The platform gates webhook calls at the front (role \+ tag check). n8n owns everything behind the webhook. How many cases a rad has done, which cases they have seen, when the gates trigger, what the decision is. The platform does not count. n8n does.

# **3\. Architecture at a glance**

The reporting platform is the only system the radiologist touches. It gates webhook calls at the front, renders every piece of UI, and exposes a decision API that n8n calls at the end. n8n lives outside, holds all the state, runs async grading on every submission, and posts milestone updates to ops Slack.

![][image1]

*Figure 1: System architecture. Platform is the gatekeeper on the way in. n8n holds the state and calls the decision API on the way out.*

# **4\. One-time setup: the 80-case pool**

Runs once. The same 80 cases are reused for every incoming Borderless candidate. The pool is not consumed.

## **4.1 The pool**

Four things define the pool, each attribute already present on the historical case record:

* 80 study\_iuid values.

* A complex / non-complex tag on each case.

* The case metadata required to activate each case on the reporting platform.

* A ground-truth report reference on each case.

## **4.2 Setup steps (n8n workflow, runs once)**

1. Download DICOMs from the server using the study\_iuid list.

2. Run each study through the anonymization script.

3. Create a dedicated client account on the reporting platform so pool cases are isolated from live clinical work.

4. Upload each anonymized study under that client, using the available metadata to activate the case.

5. Persist the case ordering for the pool. A deterministic sequence with complex cases interleaved, not clustered.

# **5\. Runtime: webhooks and state**

The reporting platform decides whether to call n8n. For every Start Reporting or Submit-and-Next action, it checks the rad's profile: role \= borderless\_rad AND incubation \= true. Only when both are true does it fire a webhook. If either is false, the normal reporting flow continues with no involvement from n8n.

Past the webhook, n8n owns everything. How many cases this rad has done, which case comes next, whether the 20-case gate triggers, whether terminal triggers. The platform carries no incubation state of its own.

![][image2]

*Figure 2: Runtime loop. The diamond at the top is platform-side gating. Webhooks only fire when both conditions are true.*

## **5.1 What n8n tracks per rad**

* The ordered list of case\_ids already assigned to this rad, so the same case is never assigned twice.

* A pointer into the pool ordering, so the next assignment is deterministic.

* The incubation\_session\_start timestamp, which is the first start\_reporting in incubation mode and anchors the 7-day window.

* The count of submitted cases, derived from the audit table. Reaching 20 and 80 is what triggers the two gates.

Because n8n owns this state, the webhook payloads from the platform stay minimal. Just the rad identity and the case that was just submitted. n8n works out what case number this is and whether a gate should fire.

## **5.2 Event: start\_reporting**

Fired when an incubation-tagged rad clicks Start Reporting. n8n returns the next unassigned case for that rad.

POST /webhook/start\_reportingExample payload (finalize contract during build):{  "rad\_id": "rad\_12345",  "session\_id": "sess\_abc",  "timestamp": "2026-04-23T09:00:00Z"}Example response:{  "action": "assign\_case",  "case\_id": "case\_042",  "study\_iuid": "1.2.840.113..."}

## **5.3 Event: submit\_and\_next**

Fired when the rad submits a report and requests the next case. n8n kicks off async grading for the submitted case, decides what to do next, and responds.

POST /webhook/submit\_and\_nextExample payload:{  "rad\_id": "rad\_12345",  "session\_id": "sess\_abc",  "submitted\_case\_id": "case\_042",  "timestamp": "2026-04-23T10:14:00Z"}Example responses:{ "action": "assign\_case", "case\_id": "case\_043", "study\_iuid": "..." }{ "action": "hold",        "reason": "grading\_in\_progress" }{ "action": "suspend" }      // 20-case gate not met, see §7{ "action": "complete" }     // 80 cases done; decision delivered via §8.3

## **5.4 What doesn't fire a webhook**

* Rad has role \!= borderless\_rad. Normal flow, no webhook.

* Rad has role \= borderless\_rad but incubation \= false (already eligible). Normal flow, no webhook. Eligible Borderless rads still use Start / Submit, but their cases come from live clinical work, not the pool.

* Stop Reporting, Logout, idle timeout, disconnect. Session ends quietly. n8n state is unchanged. Next Start Reporting resumes from the next unassigned case.

## **5.5 Platform's responsibilities on n8n responses**

| n8n response | What the platform does |
| :---- | :---- |
| assign\_case | Load the case in the rad's viewer. |
| hold | Show "Loading next case..." and re-fire submit\_and\_next after \~3 seconds. |
| suspend | Show the suspension message (§7.4). Block Start Reporting / Submit until ops unblocks. |
| complete | Acknowledge. The authoritative outcome arrives through the decision API (§8.3). The platform should trust that call, not derive anything from the "complete" response. |

# **6\. Grading pipeline**

## **6.1 Execution model**

Every submit\_and\_next webhook fires an async grading job for the case that was just submitted. Grading runs in parallel across cases. The main loop never waits for grading, except at the 20-case gate (§7).

## **6.2 Grading steps**

6. Fetch the submitted report from production DB using submitted\_case\_id.

7. Fetch the ground-truth report using the pool reference.

8. Call the LLM grading prompt with both reports.

9. Persist the result to the audit table (§10).

## **6.3 LLM output mapping**

The existing prompt returns one of four classifications. A minor tweak is needed so it maps directly to the RadPeer-aligned grades used throughout the system:

| LLM classification | Grade | Meaning |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| No change | 1 | Report concurs with ground truth |
| Minor miss \+ incidental | 2A | Minor discrepancy, not related to primary indication |
| Minor miss \+ in line of treatment | 2B | Minor discrepancy, related to primary indication |
| Major miss \+ incidental | 3A | Major discrepancy, not related to primary indication |
| Major miss \+ in line of treatment | 3B | Major discrepancy, related to primary indication |

The prompt should be updated to return the grade code (1, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B) directly, with a short rationale in a separate field. Storing both keeps the audit trail defensible if a rad disputes a grading decision.

# **7\. The 20-case quality gate**

At case 20, n8n checks whether the rad's reporting quality so far is strong enough to continue. This is an early-exit gate. If quality is weak in the first 20, we stop the incubation and have a conversation rather than letting the rad work through all 80\.

![][image3]

*Figure 3: 20-case gate. If grading is still in progress when case 20 is submitted, n8n holds the response and the platform retries after a short delay.*

## **7.1 Sync wait behaviour**

Grading is parallel and usually completes in seconds, so most grades are already persisted when the rad hits submit after case 20\. For any still in flight, n8n returns hold, the platform shows "Loading next case...", and re-fires the webhook after \~3 seconds. Worst-case user-visible wait is around 30 seconds. Case 21 is not assigned until all 20 grades are in and the gate has been evaluated.

## **7.2 The decision**

Once all 20 grades are in, n8n computes the share of cases graded 1 (no issue). If 90% or more, the gate passes and case 21 is assigned. If below 90%, n8n returns suspend, the platform shows the suspension message, and further case assignment is blocked until ops intervenes. See Figure 3 for the full path.

## **7.3 Slack alert at case 20**

Either way, pass or fail, n8n posts a short update to the ops Slack channel so the Borderless team has visibility without needing to check dashboards.

Pass:"Rad {rad\_id} cleared the 20-case gate. Quality: {x}/20 grade 1."Fail:"Rad {rad\_id} did NOT clear the 20-case gate. Quality: {x}/20 grade 1\. Needs outreach within 1 hour."

## **7.4 Suspension message (platform-rendered)**

When n8n returns suspend, the platform shows this message on the rad's screen and blocks Start Reporting / Submit until ops unblocks the account.

Your incubation is on hold.A member of the Borderless team will reach out within the hour to walkyou through where things stand and what comes next. Questions in themeantime: borderless@\[domain\].com.

# **8\. Terminal: the Borderless decision**

Incubation ends when either condition is met, whichever is first:

* The rad completes all 80 cases.

* 7 calendar days have elapsed since the rad's first Start Reporting in incubation mode.

At the terminal point, n8n evaluates four metrics, determines one of four outcomes, calls the platform's decision API, and posts to ops Slack. Quality is a hard gate. If quality is not met, the rad is blocked regardless of the other three.

*Figure 4: Borderless decision flow. Quality is the hard gate. P/R/C count decides the branch when quality is met. n8n calls the platform API with the outcome. The platform handles everything downstream.*

## **8.1 The four metrics**

| Metric | Threshold | Source |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Quality | \>=90% of submitted cases graded as 1 | Audit table (from §6) |
| Punctuality | Late login on \<=10% of committed days | Slot-commitment system |
| Regularity | Missed \<=10% of committed days | Slot-commitment system |
| Consistency | \>=90% of attended days at full committed duration | Slot-commitment system |

## **8.2 Borderless decision matrix**

Quality is a hard gate. If Quality is not met, the rad is always blocked. If Quality is met, the count of passing P/R/C metrics determines the outcome.

| Quality | P/R/C passing | Outcome | Notification |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Yes | 3 of 3 | ELIGIBLE | Eligibility |
| Yes | 2 of 3 | ELIGIBLE | Eligibility |
| Yes | 1 of 3 | BLOCK\_B | Block B |
| Yes | 0 of 3 | BLOCK\_B | Block B |
| No | 1 or more of 3 | BLOCK\_A | Block A |
| No | 0 of 3 | BLOCK\_C | Block C |

Rule precedence. Evaluate Quality first. If No and all three P/R/C failed, outcome is BLOCK\_C. If No with any P/R/C passing, outcome is BLOCK\_A. If Yes, count P/R/C and branch per the table.

## **8.3 Decision API (n8n → platform)**

The platform exposes a single endpoint that n8n calls when incubation concludes. Used for both terminal paths. Path one: 80-cases-done, called during submit\_and\_next handling. Path two: 7-day-timeout, called from an n8n scheduled job when the rad is offline. This is the authoritative channel for the outcome.

POST /api/incubation/decisionExample payload (finalize contract during build):{  "rad\_id": "rad\_12345",  "outcome": "ELIGIBLE | BLOCK\_A | BLOCK\_B | BLOCK\_C",  "trigger": "cases\_completed | days\_elapsed",  "evaluated\_at": "2026-04-30T11:22:00Z"}

What the platform does with each outcome:

* ELIGIBLE. Flip incubation \= false, open the Borderless earnings page on the rad's profile, show the eligibility notification (§8.5).

* BLOCK\_A / BLOCK\_B / BLOCK\_C. Show the matching block notification (§8.5). Keep the rad on the standard network. Notify Borderless ops.

## **8.4 Slack alert at terminal**

At terminal, whether the rad completed 80 cases or hit the 7-day timeout, n8n posts a summary to ops Slack with the outcome and the underlying numbers.

"Rad {rad\_id}, outcome: {ELIGIBLE | BLOCK\_A | BLOCK\_B | BLOCK\_C} Quality: {x}% grade 1 P: {pass/fail}  |  R: {pass/fail}  |  C: {pass/fail} Trigger: {cases\_completed | days\_elapsed}"

## **8.5 Notification copy**

Four notifications, one per decision. The platform renders these in-app after n8n calls the decision API. The voice is Borderless: direct, confident, respectful of the reader. No over-explaining, no softening the standard.

### **Eligibility (ELIGIBLE)**

***Sample notification:*** You cleared the assessment.Your Borderless access is live on your profile. The earnings page is open,and cases from our partner hospitals will begin arriving in your queueduring your committed slots.Welcome to the network.

### **Block A (quality bar not met, some availability met)**

***Sample notification:*** The quality bar for Borderless was not met in this assessment.We hold this line because the hospitals on the other end are trusting usto stand behind every radiologist we send them. That is not a positionwe soften.A member of our team will walk you through where our panel differed,read by read. You remain on the standard network. If you would like toreassess in three months, we are open to it.

### **Block B (quality met, availability not met)**

***Sample notification:*** Your reads met the bar. That is not in question.Borderless is built on slot commitment. The hospitals we work with plancoverage around the radiologists we schedule, and the availability wesaw in this assessment does not match the reliability the tier commits to.You remain on the standard network. If your schedule opens up forstronger commitment, we welcome a second look in a few months.

### **Block C (neither quality nor availability met)**

***Sample notification:*** Neither bar was met in this assessment, quality or availability.A member of our team will walk you through where things landed, in detail.You remain on the standard network. The door to reassess is not closed.

# **9\. What you see as a radiologist in incubation**

The experience from the rad's side. What appears on your screen, what you click, and what happens at each milestone. If this does not match what a rad actually sees during testing, the build is not done.

## **Day 1, when you start**

You log in within your committed slot and click Start Reporting. The first case opens in your viewer. You read it, write your report, and click Submit and Load Next. The next case appears. You keep going.

When you are done for the day, you click Take Break or Logout. Nothing dramatic happens. Your session ends quietly, and the next time you start reporting, the next unseen case is ready.

## **At case 20**

This is your first checkpoint. After you submit case 20, you may see a brief "Loading next case..." screen for up to 30 seconds while your early work is being reviewed. Then one of two things happens:

* Case 21 opens and you continue as normal.

* You see a message telling you your incubation has been paused and a team member will reach out within an hour.

That is the only visible change at case 20\. You do not need to do anything.

## **Cases 21 through 79**

Normal reporting. Same as any session. Cases keep arriving each time you are online. No checkpoints, no messages, no interruptions.

## **At case 80 (or at the 7-day mark)**

This is the decision point. After you submit case 80, or when your 7-day window closes, whichever comes first, you see one of four outcomes:

* Eligible for Borderless. Your access is now live, the earnings page opens on your profile, and the base pay for the initial 80 cases will be credited to your account.

* A block note, for a specific reason (A, B, or C). You are not moving forward right now. A team member follows up to walk you through what we saw.

If the 7-day window closes before you reach 80 cases, the decision is made on whatever you completed. You do not have to open the app to trigger it. The next time you log in, the outcome is already there.

# **10\. Data storage and audit**

All per-case grading decisions persist to an audit table the Borderless ops team can query. Schema suggestion below.

| Field | Type | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| rad\_id | string | The incubating radiologist |
| incubation\_session\_start | timestamp | First start\_reporting in incubation mode (anchors the 7-day window) |
| case\_id | string | Pool case identifier |
| case\_number | int | 1 to 80, position in this rad's sequence (n8n derives this) |
| submitted\_at | timestamp | When rad hit submit |
| submitted\_report\_ref | string | Pointer to report in prod DB |
| ground\_truth\_ref | string | Pointer to ground-truth report |
| llm\_classification | string | Raw LLM output |
| grade | string | Mapped grade: 1, 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B |
| llm\_rationale | text | LLM explanation, for dispute defensibility |
| graded\_at | timestamp | When grading completed |
